calendar.matters:
Petition For Aproval Of Settlement Agreements Relating To Beneficial Interests Of Regina Hagan And Bernard Hagan, Jr. In The I Trust
Off Calendar Oct-16-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-17-301371 In re: Bernard P. Hagan and
PTR-21-304383 In re: Roseanne Bertucelli as Trustee of The I Trust

Appearances: Jeffrey Loew appeared by zoom for Will Monroe (beneficiary).
Jessica Neugebauer appeared by zoom for Bernard Hagan (beneficiary).
Gabrielle Korte appeared by zoom for Regina Hagan (beneficiary).
Robert Cross appeared by zoom for Paula Bennett, Maryann Bertucelli and Peter Renstrom (respondents).

The court noted that the settlement agreement has language in it about how it is going to become "null and void" if not approved by the court by a certain date, and that the date has passed. Mr. Cross stated that they had several continuances, that the settling parties have agreed to waive court approval, that they are working on it now, that Will's share was distributed by the trustee, and that they are still hoping to speak with Judge Sugiyama. Mr. Cross also stated that dismissals by Mr. Monroe have not been received as of yet and asked the court to take the matters off calendar as moot. Mr. Loew stated that he believes his client is willing to sign the dismissals. No one objects to the court taking the matters off calendar as moot. The Petitions for Approval of Settlement Agreements (2), and the Petition for Instructions Re: Distribution, etc. are off calendar as moot.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Petition For Relief For Breach Of Trust; Breach Of Trust Trustee De Son Tort/De Facto Trustee; Complaint For Fraudulent Concealment **Filing Date Backdated To 4/1/21 Per Ex Parte Order Filed 5/19/21**
Off Calendar Mar-14-2022 Continued To Jul-11-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gabrielle Korte, via ZOOM, for Regina Hagan, Petitioner; Robert Cross, via ZOOM, for Respondents; Alex Weingarten, via ZOOM, for Paula Bennett, Beneficiary; Bernard Hagan, via ZOOM, Beneficiary.

The court notes a recent filing and asks if a request for an evidentiary hearing might be expected. Mr. Cross reports that he intends to file a response soon and advises that there is an appeal regarding the insurance policy and requests a 90-120-day continuance to allow time to resolve the appeals process. Ms. Korte advises that this is the first time she has heard news regarding the appeal but that it is reasonable to continue this matter in light of the preceding. Mr. Weingarten asks the court to stay this matter until a decision has been made regarding the appeal. The court advises Mr. Weingarten that it will not rule on his request to stay this matter without it first being formally filed with the court.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Relief of Breach of Trust, etc., is continued to Jul-11-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.
